summary: Tokyo is a dazzling megacity that seamlessly blends ancient tradition with futuristic innovation. World-class cuisine at every price point, impeccable public transport, dazzling neighbourhoods each with a distinct personality, and some of the safest streets in the world.
neighbourhoods: Shinjuku (entertainment, Kabukicho, department stores), Shibuya (crossing, youth fashion), Harajuku (Takeshita Street, Meiji Shrine), Asakusa (Senso-ji, old Tokyo), Akihabara (electronics, anime), Ginza (luxury shopping), Roppongi (nightlife, museums), Yanaka (old townscape)
must_do: Shibuya Crossing at rush hour, Senso-ji Temple in Asakusa, teamLab digital art museum, Tsukiji outer market breakfast, day trip to Nikko or Kamakura, sumo wrestling match (January/May/September), Shinjuku Golden Gai bar hopping, Hamarikyu Gardens
transport_tips: Get a Suica or Pasmo IC card for all trains, subways and buses. JR Pass for long-distance travel. Tokyo Metro day pass (~$10 AUD) for unlimited metro rides. Taxis expensive - avoid for long distances. Airport: Narita Express or Limousine Bus (~$25-35 AUD to central Tokyo).
safety_notes: Tokyo is extremely safe. Typhoon season July-October. Earthquake preparedness: follow hotel evacuation signs. Keep valuables in hotel safe. Japan has strict drug laws.
budget_notes: Budget: $120-150 AUD/day. Mid-range: $200-300 AUD/day. Luxury: $400+ AUD/day. Convenience store meals $5-10 AUD. Ramen $10-15 AUD. Restaurant set lunch $15-25 AUD. Accommodation from $50 (capsule hotel) to $300+ (luxury hotel).

sample_day_blocks.activity: Tsukiji outer market sushi breakfast + Hamarikyu Gardens
teamLab Borderless or Planets digital art
Shinjuku Omoide Yokocho (Memory Lane) yakitori + Golden Gai
